front.title: Blackwood Fetishes
front.description:
A pair of wooden figurines, carved from blackwood and worn smooth with age and use. A whitish shaft has been driven into the top of each figurine’s head, and the eyes and forehead of each are stained a rusty, reddish color.

The figurines resist all mundane attempts to damage them. Such attempts might mar them slightly, but their forms hold fast.

content.text: When you **_mark a figurine’s eyes with blood and likewise mark your own eyelids_**, you see through the eyes of the figurine whenever you close your eyes, for as long as the blood remains.

When you **_anoint a figurine with your own blood and then sleep in its presence_**, roll +CON: **on a 10+**, you dream lucidly of the spirit in the figurine, which speaks an archaic but intelligible dialect—it might be persuaded to reveal its name, or the name of its fellow spirit, or the method of calling them forth; **on a 7-9**, you dream of the spirit, but the dream is fleeting, strange, and disorienting—ask the GM what you learn of the spirit.
When you **_learn the name of one of the bound spirits_**, or the sign that draws them forth and binds them, mark 1:
When you **_make the last mark_**, you unlock the mysteries of the Blackwood Fetishes and can use Call Forth and Command (see reverse).
